goal: Polish Chinese website copy in WeChat public account style

summary.title: Simplify Chinese website copy
summary.what: Rewrote the Chinese website copy to be more conversational and closer to a WeChat public-account style. The change simplifies the Chinese landing/docs/spec/blog/comparison copy and fully rewrites the Chinese repo-memory essay around a clearer hook, concrete pain, the Redis example, and practical Mainline workflow.
summary.why: The prior Chinese copy was accurate but too protocol-heavy and mixed too many English terms. The user wanted more plain-language copy that reads naturally for a Chinese technical audience.
summary.user_goal: Make the Chinese copy more plain-spoken and closer to a WeChat public-account style.

decisions.chose: Use a direct pain-first Chinese style instead of literal technical translation.
decisions.rationale: A WeChat-style article should open with a relatable problem, use shorter paragraphs, and explain technical terms through concrete scenarios.

decisions.chose: Keep necessary product terms such as agent, repo, intent, Git, and Mainline, but explain the idea as project memory.
decisions.rationale: The target reader likely understands the English engineering terms, but the value proposition should still be readable without decoding protocol language.
The user specifically asked to improve the Chinese wording; English and Spanish behavior and routing are unchanged.

rejected.alternative: Make the Chinese copy fully localized with no English engineering terms.
rejected.reason: Terms like AI agent, repo, PR, Git, Redis, and Mainline are natural in this audience and removing them would make the copy less precise.
The blog article carried most of the translated, formal tone, so improving only the hero would leave the main Chinese content inconsistent.

review_notes: Verified with npm run build; Astro check reported 0 errors and 0 warnings.
The prior open risk about native-speaker review is improved for Chinese copy but not fully resolved because it also covers Spanish copy.

behavioral_changes: Chinese landing/docs/spec/blog/comparison copy now uses more plain-language positioning around project memory and avoiding repeated engineering mistakes.
The Chinese repo-memory essay now has a pain-first WeChat-style structure with shorter paragraphs and a clearer Redis example.
